bodyHtml:
Home/FHA Loan Requirements Florida
FHA Loan Requirements Florida

FHA loan requirements in Florida

Wondering if you qualify? FHA loans in Florida need just a 580 credit score, 3.5% down, and steady income. Here's everything you need to meet the requirements and get approved.

Get My Free Rate QuoteCall __PHONE_DISPLAY__
580+ credit score3.5% downDTI up to 43–50%First-time buyer friendly

Get your free FHA quote

No obligation. See your rate & payment in minutes.

🔒 Your information is secure. By submitting you agree to be contacted about FHA loan options. This is not a commitment to lend.

Thank you!

An FHA mortgage specialist will reach out shortly with your personalized Florida rate quote.

Qualifying

FHA loan requirements in Florida

FHA loans have more flexible guidelines than most conventional mortgages, which is why they're so popular with first-time buyers in Florida. Here are the core requirements you'll need to meet.

  • 1
    Credit score of 580 or higherQualifies you for the 3.5% minimum down payment. Scores of 500–579 may still qualify with 10% down.
  • 2
    3.5% down paymentFunds can come from savings or a gift from family. Sellers may also contribute up to 6% toward closing costs.
  • 3
    Debt-to-income ratioTypically up to 43%–50% of gross monthly income, depending on compensating factors like reserves.
  • 4
    Steady, verifiable incomeA reliable two-year employment history strengthens your application and approval odds.
  • 5
    Primary residence & FHA appraisalThe home must be your primary residence (1–4 units) and pass an FHA appraisal for safety and value.
  • 6
    Within FHA loan limitsYour loan must fall under your county's FHA limit — from $541,287 in most Florida counties up to $990,150 in Monroe.

FHA mortgage insurance (MIP) requirements

Every FHA loan in Florida includes mortgage insurance. There's a one-time upfront premium of 1.75% of the loan amount (usually financed into the loan) plus an annual premium of about 0.55% paid monthly.

If you put down less than 10%, the annual premium stays for the life of the loan. With 10% or more down, it drops off after 11 years. Many Florida homeowners later refinance into a conventional loan once they reach 20% equity to remove mortgage insurance.

Documents you'll typically need

Be ready with recent pay stubs, two years of W-2s or tax returns, two months of bank statements, photo ID, and details on any other debts. Having these ready helps us move from application to pre-approval quickly.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What are the FHA loan requirements in Florida?+
You generally need a credit score of 580+ for 3.5% down (or 500–579 with 10% down), a debt-to-income ratio typically up to 43%–50%, steady verifiable income, and the home must be your primary residence and pass an FHA appraisal.
What credit score do I need for an FHA loan in Florida?+
A credit score of 580 or higher qualifies you for the 3.5% minimum down payment. Borrowers with scores between 500 and 579 may still qualify with a 10% down payment.
Can I get an FHA loan in Florida as a first-time buyer?+
Yes. FHA loans are one of the most popular options for first-time buyers in Florida thanks to the low down payment, flexible credit guidelines, and compatibility with state down payment assistance programs.
Do FHA loans in Florida require mortgage insurance?+
Yes. FHA loans include a 1.75% upfront premium (usually financed) and an annual premium of about 0.55% paid monthly. With under 10% down it lasts the life of the loan; with 10%+ down it ends after 11 years.
